steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: First, notice that both terms contain the same absolute‑value expression $|4-x|$. Add the coefficients: $$2|4-x|+3|4-x| = (2+3)|4-x| = 5|4-x|.$$ Then set this equal to 25: $$5|4-x| = 25.$$ Divide both sides by 5 to isolate the absolute value: $$|4-x| = 5.$$

steps.title: Solve the absolute‑value equation
steps.content:
The equation $|4-x|=5$ means $4-x=5$ or $4-x=-5$. Solve each:
$$4-x=5 \Rightarrow\ -x = 1 \Rightarrow\ x = -1$$
$$4-x=-5 \Rightarrow\ -x = -9 \Rightarrow\ x = 9.$$ The positive solution is $x=9$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
